{{Quote|His Mace Windship will forever haunt Squibbish poems and jingles, and future fuzzlings will hear of the valiant Knight with his koovy purplish blade.|King Ebareebaveebeedee, dedicating a statue to Mace Windu following the Battle of Skor II|<ref name="Core Edition 14:9:01" />}}
The '''Battle of Skor II''' was a clash between the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] and the [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Confederacy of Independent Systems]] on [[Skor II]], the home [[Planet/Legends|planet]] of the [[Squib/Legends|Squibs]]. Taking place in [[22 BBY/Legends|22 BBY]], a contingent of [[Clone trooper/Legends|clone troopers]] under the command of [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Mace Windu/Legends|Mace Windu]] and [[Clone Commander/Legends|Clone Commander]] [[Cody/Legends|CC-2224]] succeeded in liberating [[Metrobig City]], which had been occupied by the [[Droid/Legends|droid]] [[Separatist Droid Army/Legends|army]]. In doing so, they thwarted the Separatist plan to mine the nearby [[Rimma Trade Route/Legends|Rimma Trade Route]] from Skor II.

==Prelude==
The [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Confederacy of Independent Systems]] occupied [[Skor II]]'s [[Metrobig Interplanetary Blastport]], intending to use the [[Planet/Legends|planet]]—the [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] of the [[Squib/Legends|Squibs]]—as a base to place [[Proximity mine/Legends|proximity mines]] throughout the outer [[Rimma Trade Route/Legends|Rimma Trade Route]]. In response, the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] dispatched a contingent of [[Clone trooper/Legends|clone troopers]] under the command of [[High Jedi General/Legends|High Jedi General]] [[Mace Windu/Legends|Mace Windu]]<ref name="Core Edition 14:9:01" /> and [[Clone Marshal Commander/Legends|Marshal Commander]] [[Cody/Legends|CC-2224]] to retake the world.<ref name="Vehicles Collection 32">[[Star Wars: The Official Starships & Vehicles Collection 32|''Star Wars: The Official Starships & Vehicles Collection'' 32]]</ref>

==The battle==
Windu and CC-2224 led their [[Cloning/Legends|clone]] [[Trooper/Legends|troopers]] in recapturing Skor II from the occupational force of [[Battle droid/Legends|battle droids]] from the [[Separatist Droid Army/Legends|Separatist Droid Army]], resulting in the liberation of [[Metrobig City]].<ref name="Core Edition 14:9:01" />

==Aftermath==
[[File:Mace_Windu_statue_on_Skor_II.jpg|thumb|right|King Ebareebaveebeedee dedicates a statue to Windu.]]
While Windu left Skor II, a clone trooper peacekeeping force was left on-world to monitor Separatist activity. [[King/Legends|King]] [[Ebareebaveebeedee]] of the Squibs dedicated a [[Bronzium/Legends|bronzium]] statue of Windu, whom he proclaimed as a "[[Big Time Hero of Beyond-Squib Eliteness]]," in Metrobig [[City/Legends|City]].<ref name="Core Edition 14:9:01" />

==Behind the scenes==
Although ''[[Republic HoloNet News Core Edition 14:9:01]]'' implied that the Battle of Skor II occurred in [[21 BBY/Legends|21 BBY]],<ref name="Core Edition 14:9:01" /> [[Star Wars: The Official Starships & Vehicles Collection 32|''The Official Starships & Vehicles Collection 32'']]—which also incorporated [[Commander/Legends|Commander]] CC-2224's involvement in the battle, originally implied in the novel ''[[Labyrinth of Evil]]''<ref name="Labyrinth of Evil" />—placed it prior to the [[Battle of Christophsis/Legends|Battle of Christophsis]],<ref name="Vehicles Collection 32" /> which ''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]'' established as taking place within seven weeks of the start of the war.<ref name="ERC"/>
